texts.full_text: could be licensed to manufacturers, who would create a database of results for each of the chips that ships out of the factory. No manufacturers have approached Swanson's team yet, he said. The research was first presented last month. Steven Swanson, an associate professor at the University of California at San Diego, discussed research on authenticating NAND flash at the Flash Memory Summit in Santa Clara, California. The system uses "physically unclonable functions" (PUFs), or variations in manufacturing that are unique to each element of each flash chip. Swanson described one PUF that his team has worked with, called Program Disturb. It uses a type of manufacturing flaw that doesn't affect normal operation but causes problems under test conditions. Data is written and erased from NAND flash through changes in the states of each cell, which are applied by sending a voltage through the cell. If a cell is rewritten many times in a row, the voltage can bleed into an adjacent cell so much that the adjacent cell also changes its state. The order in which cells are modified prevents this from happening in normal operation, Swanson said. In the test, the number of repetitions it takes to disturb the neighboring cell depends on the thickness of the barriers between the cells and other factors, which vary from one chip to another, Swanson said. The Program Disturb test counts the number of repetitions needed to cause the cell next door to change its state, typically hundreds or thousands. It can be run again later to see if that number matches the original result. The UCSD researchers even considered the possibility that a highly skilled and motivated hacker, such as an enemy government, could fool this test. texts.full_text: CBT can help manage delusions and hallucinations, while social skills training can improve interpersonal relationships and social functioning. 4. Supporting Medication Adherence: Therapies like motivational interviewing can help improve adherence to medication, which is crucial for managing schizophrenia effectively. 5. Improving Quality of Life: Therapies that focus on life vocational training, and social integration can significantly improve the quality of life for individuals with schizophrenia. ### Case Stories Let's consider a case stories to illustrate how these co-therapies might be applied: #### Case Story 1: John Background: John, a 28-year-old male, has been diagnosed with schizophrenia for five years. He experiences auditory hallucinations and paranoid delusions, which significantly impact his daily life and social interactions. Therapy Approach: - Medication Management: John is on antipsychotic medication to manage his symptoms. - Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy (CBT): To address his delusions and hallucinations. - Social Skills Training: To improve his social interactions and reduce social isolation. - Family Therapy: To support his family in understanding and coping with his condition. Outcome: Over time, John's delusions and hallucinations have reduced, and he has made significant progress in his social interactions. His family has also become more and understanding. #### Case Story 2: Sarah Background: Sarah, a 32-year-old female, has been diagnosed with schizophrenia and has struggled with medication adherence and social withdrawal. Therapy Approach: - Medication Management: Sarah is on a regimen of antipsychotic medication. - Motivational Interviewing: To address her reluctance towards medication adherence. - Art Therapy: To provide a creative outlet and help her express her feelings and experiences. - Vocational Training: To help her regain confidence and find meaningful employment. Outcome: Sarah has become more engaged with her treatment and has started to participate in vocational training programs. Her adherence to medication has improved, and she has shown a willingness to engage more socially. ### Conclusion Whil

texts.full_text: 2>new I was expecting new to be overwritten since >> is not used. But in the output file I got both contents appended. Why is it so? A: TL;DR bash opens and truncates all involved files before anything is written to them. stdout and stderr are both sent to new because bash has already truncated the file (twice) when ls starts printing. This is how bash prepares/handles I/O redirection. when you ask for a command to be redirected, bash opens all involved files (in this case, `new`) and truncates them before any output from the command i

texts.full_text: nvolving the right occipital lobe. Total removal of the tumor (pilocytic astrocytoma, WHO grade 1) relieved her migraine completely. It has been previously reported that brain tumors are associated with migraine. But this is the first report of occipital pilocytic astrocytoma presenting as migraine with aura. As for other brain tumors manifesting migrai...as a symptom, they have mostly occurred in supratentorial locations and were often accompanied by seizures or focal neurological deficits. In contrast to our case where the tumor was located exclusively within the occipital lobe without any surrounding edema or mass effect on adjacent brain structures. The complete removal of this pilocytic astrocytoma resulted not only in relief from migraine symptoms but also a significant improvement in visual acuity and reduction in frequency. The pathophysiology underlying these observations is unclear, however it has been suggested that the presence of tumor tissue may lead to localized inflammation or release of vasoactive substances which could stimulate trigeminal nerve activation. texts.full_text: of modules over the path algebra of a quiver [@Goodman:96]. A graph $X$ with a specified orientation is represented by a matrix in which the row indices correspond to the source vertices of $X$ and the column indices correspond to the sink vertices. The (i,j)-entry of this matrix is $1$ if there is an arc from $i$ to $j$, and $0$ otherwise. This matrix determines a representation of $X$, and we consider only representations that are indecomposable. An oriented graph $X$ is said to be indecomposable if it does not contain a copy of the oriented cycle of length 2, with its orientation reversed. If we take the transpose of a matrix associated with an indecomposable graph, we obtain another matrix that also represents an oriented graph. We consider the set of matrices associated with oriented graphs that are indecomposable. This set of matrices forms an algebraic object called a quiver. We will assume that this quiver has no oriented cycles of length $2$. If there were such oriented cycles, then the entire quiver would be decomposable. The quiver is the combinatorial object of interest. In this paper we enumerate the conjugacy classes of Coxeter elements in a

texts.full_text: s in history” at the University of Leicester. The range of viewpoints and methodologies on display was slightly dizzying, but we came away with some fairy niggling questions:… Previous blogs have touched upon the ways in which the Imperial government in Loyas used maps to assert its power over territories such as India or Burma. However, this workshop made us think more deeply about how these islands were represented and what that representation meant for their historical development. The concept of "islands in history" refers to small-scale entities like archipelagos which have often been overlooked by historians due to the focus on larger landmasses or empires. These smaller territories, despite being geographically isolated from major continents, played significant roles within global networks and were deeply intertwined with colonialism. During our workshop at the University of Leicester, we encountered various perspectives that highlighted how these islands have been shaped by their unique historical contexts—such as trade routes or cultural exchanges. The diverse methodologies presented allowed us to appreciate different approaches in studying such regions: from archaeological findings and environmental studies to economic analyses and social histories. One recurring theme was the importance of maps, cartography, and visual representations that played a crucial role not only for navigation but also for asserting sovereignty over these islands during colonial times. Maps were tools used by European powers like Britain or France in their imperial ventures; they helped define borders between territories under different control—often arbitrarily drawn without regard to local knowledge

Create a Filter: You can create a filter in Google Analytics to exclude traffic from specific IP addresses or domains that are known to send spam. Go to Admin > Filters and click on the "+ Add Filter" button. 2. Use a Referral Exclusion List: This list allows you to specify which domains should be ignored as referrers. You can find this under Property Settings in Google analytics. 3. Block at the Server Level: If you have access to your server settings, you can block traffic from known spammy IP addresses or domains. this can be done using tools like .htaccess for Apache servers or by configuring your firewall. 4. texts.full_text: a short answer, is the fact you don't concat the values into the query string yourself. Making it less prone to sql injection. A: Like many other PHP users you are taking escaping wrong. You taking it as a some sort of magic wand which makes some "evil characters" "safe". This is wrong idea. though prepared statements can be taken as a sort of such a magic wand, escaping is not a synonym for "SQL injection protection". It is a merely string syntax rule - no more, no less. Is it necessary to also use $mysqli->real_escape_string($num); It is irrelevant question. To escape or not to escape decision have to be bound to SQl, not to the data source or any validations: real_escape_string() have to be used for the sql strings, i.e. parts of the query enclosed in quotes. Have to be used unconditionally, despite of whatever previous manipulations. For the any other part of the query real_escape_string() being completely useless. An explanation: Data validation rules can be changed. While SQL building rules have to be explicit and unconditional. To make a developer never ask himself a question like this. In fact, it's completely different matters: data validation and query building. Why keep in mind such details and build the query accordingly? Why not to build the query based on some set of general purpose rules, irrelevant of the data nature at all? So, to your question again: if you are adding your data to the query as is, without quotes, real_escape_string() going to be completely useless in this case, but casting/validation become essential. if you are adding your data to the query using prepared statement, real_escape_string() going to be completely useless and even harmful. if you are adding your data to the query in quotes - you ought to do real_escape_string() in this case. it is also worth to mention that if you are adding your data to the query as a part of SQL language - as an identifier or an SQL keyword - real_escape_string() is completely useless too, as well as prepared statement. texts.full_text: solve for the direction-dependent amplitude and phase variations in the data and to update the shapelet model for Cas A. To mitigate artefacts due to Cygnus A, present about 30 degrees away, we simultaneously solved for direction-dependent gains towards Cas A and Cygnus A, and subtracted the latter in the visibility data using a shapelet model. We then imaged the source using the [casa]{} imager (<http://casa.nrao.edu/>) and constructed a composite sky model from the image by fitting for point and shapelet sources [see e.g., @Yatawatta:2011]. We iterated over this scheme ([bbs]{} calibration $\rightarrow$ imaging $\rightarrow$ skymodel $\rightarrow$ calibration) to obtain high-resolution continuum images

In output 1from the MC, I rot-5 the digits in place and write to a destination and output 2 with theunaltered version, there has to be an additional memorycost associatedwith that but I assume it's just the space required for the columnto beduplicated. *All the downstream componentsuse thesame memory address until you introduce asynchronouscomponents (Sort, aggregate, etc). When those hit the data flow, then that buffer ends and the data is copied from memory space 1 into memory space 2. Which sounds good from the perspective of "I just reduced the number of columns from 100 to 10" but you've fractured your total memoryspace between "before async component" and "after async". Instead of being able to run 100 buffers at once, you're at 30 before and 70 after and that memory copy in between is an expensive operation. If you want to reduce memory usage, architect your packages to use less ;) You can use less byavoiding the asynchronous transformations. Only pull in the columns you need. If you have a lookup, write a query explicitly enumerating the needed columnsinsteadof clicking thetable in the drop down list. If you have a lookup, look at restricting the depth of the rows returned. If you only need tolookup current values, inthe query above, makeit WHERE ISCurrent = CAST(1 AS bit); Watch your data types. Importing from a file, don't be lazy and start withstrings only to convert toa data type later(unless you have questionable data cleanliness) Watch your datatypes too! texts.full_text: The beneficial effects humidity treatments can have on the properties of mesoporous silica (MPS) thin-film materials are significant. Humidity treatment, when applied appropriately to these structures: 1. Enhances Porosimetry: It allows for a more uniform distribution and stabilization of pore sizes within MPS films by facilitating water molecule adsorption into narrow pores or at defect sites in the material's structure. 2. Improves Mechanical Integrity: - By reducing residual stresses: Humidity treatment can lead to swelling, which helps release internal mechanical stress that accumulates during synthesis. - Through densification without collapse of pore structures: Controlled exposure enables partial healing and sintering at defect sites or grain boundaries while maintaining the overall mesoporous architecture. 3. Optimizes Surface Properties: Exposure alters surface chemistry through hydroxylation, which can improve adhesion to substrates as well as enhance compatibility with functionalization processes for applications such as sensing elements in microelectromechanical systems (MEMS). 4. Stabilises Microstructure Over Time and Usage Cycles: - Repeated controlled humidity exposure conditions the material against degradation from environmental moisture, thereby extending operational lifetimes. 5. Facilitates Tailored Property Engineering: By adjusting parameters like duration or relative air saturation levels during treatment phases; researchers can fine-tune specific attributes required for targeted applications in catalysis support materials photonics devices drug delivery carriers etcetera In summary, the application of humidity treatments to mesoporous silica films is a powerful tool that not only stabilizes these delicate structures but also enhances their performance across various critical parameters necessary for

Whether it's a celebration of friendship, family, or romance, these mittens embody the spirit of love in all its forms. Embrace the joy of free love and the beauty of connection with these special mittens. Perfect for crafting enthusiasts looking to add a heartfelt project to their collection, or for gifting to someone special during this festive season. So grab your needles and yarn, and let's knit our way to a world filled with love and warmth! Happy crafting! 🧶❤️️ --- Materials Needed: - Worsted weight yarn in colors of your choice - Size US 7 knitting needles - Stitch markers - Tapestry needle Skills Required: - Basic knitting techniques (knit, purl, cast on, bind off) - Ability to follow a chart or written instructions Pattern Overview: 1. Cast On: Start with the cuff and work in the round using a rib stitch for elasticity. 2. Mitten Body: Follow the charted pattern, which includes a charming heart motif that represents the spirit of love. 3. Thumb Gusset: Create a thumb gusset for comfort and fit. 4. Decrease and Finish: Shape the top of the mitten and bind off securely. Notes: - Customize your mittens with your choice of colors to match any style or theme. - This pattern is a great way to practice chart reading and cable techniques. texts.full_text: I agree that it's worth getting your hands on this device now rather than waiting and potentially missing out or paying more later—especially since minor criticisms about its features pale in comparison with the overall experience you can enjoy right away after purchase! User profiles are a great feature, too; I'm glad they're included. The lack of Flash is actually quite refreshing for me personally because my browser settings already block it due to security concerns and performance issues on many sites that use heavy amounts-of animation or video content anyway so having an official endorsement from Apple feels like validation! If Apple had included flash support, it'd be very unlikely that they'd have provided a flash blocker, so I'm actually very grateful that they made this decision. In my opinion, flash is only good for two things: games and videos; and I tend to avoid both of those things when I surf anyways. As far as the keyboard goes, I can actually type on the damn thing almost as fast as I can my laptop keyboard, but that's probably because I'm so awesome.

OlHere's a completed version of your text with proper formatting and citations: Oleosins from Arabidopsis thaliana: Expression in Escherichia coli, Purification, and Functional Properties The interfacial behavior of oleosins, the most abundant proteins found on seeds' oil bodies [1], was investigated using the pendant drop method at water/oil interfaces. Their performance was compared to that of two benchmark food proteins with contrasting emulsifying properties: β-casein (highly surface active) and lysozyme (poor surfactant). Recombinant high molecular weight (rS3) and low molecular weight (rS4) oleosins, both comprising N-terminal histidine tags for purification purposes [2], were expressed in BL21(DE3), purified to electrophoretic homogeneity using immobilized metal affinity chromatography (IMAC) under native conditions. The interfacial tension measurements revealed that oleosin rS3 showed superior performance compared with recombinant oleisin of lower molecular weight (rS4), as well as β-casein, in reducing the oil/water interface tensions. This difference can be attributed to their distinct amino acid compositions and secondary structures [1]. If there it is a draw after five rounds then the both users will have to roll one die to determine the winner. from random import randint from time import sleep import time import sys import random import operator total_score2 = 0 total_score1 = 0 rounds = 0 playerOnePoints = 0 playerTwoPoints = 0 print("*****************Welcome To The DICE Game*******************") print("Please enter 'n' if you are a new user and 'e' if you are a exsiting user and enter 's' to display scores") ens=input("") while ens != ("e") and ens != ("n") and ens != ("s"): # if anything else but these characters are entered it will loop until it is correct print("Please enter 'n' if you are a new user and 'e' if you are a exsiting user and enter 's' to display scores") ens = input() if ens == "s": s = open("scores.txt","r") file_content = s.read().splitlines() users_points = {i.split()[0]: int(i.split()[2]) for i in file_content} best_player = max(users_points.items(), key=operator.itemgetter(1))[0] print("LeaderBoard: ") print("\n") print('player with maximum points is {}, this player has {} points.'.format(best_player, users_points.get(best_player)))
